name: Fixer's Kit
tagline: If it has moving parts, you can break it — or make it worse.
description: Precision tools, salvaged components, and diagnostic datapads for anyone who keeps machines running past their expiration date.

benefits.description: Standard field tools. Sufficient for most repairs and modifications in the field.
Once per scene, attempt to repair a Broken item with scavenged parts. On a success, it functions for the rest of the scene.
Standard field tools.
Grants +1 Effect Tier on Tech rolls to diagnose or repair ship systems and vehicles.
Once per scene, attempt to repair a Broken item with scavenged parts.
After any encounter involving destroyed vehicles or droids, extract components worth 1d6×10 credits.

name: Medic's Satchel
tagline: You're not a doctor. You just play one in warzones.
description: Trauma supplies, bacta gel, and enough stimulants to keep a squad operational long after they should've stopped moving.

benefits.description: Standard trauma kits. Consumable — each handles one casualty.
Once per encounter, use a Maneuver (not Action) to clear one Trauma from an adjacent ally.
Standard trauma kits.
Allows extended care procedures. +1 Effect Tier on Medicine rolls in non-combat situations.
Once per encounter, use a Maneuver to clear one Trauma from an adjacent ally.
Once per encounter, inject an ally with a combat stimulant. They ignore Wound penalties until end of the round (then gain [Fatigued]).

name: Scout Package
description: Lightweight recon gear for characters who operate ahead of the group, through hostile terrain, or entirely off the map.

benefits.description: Long-range optical scanner. Identify targets and terrain up to 2 km away.
Rations, filtration tabs, emergency shelter. Negates environmental Survival penalties for 72 hours.
When you hold your Action, you may declare Overwatch. The first enemy that moves into your line of sight triggers a free Ranged attack.
Long-range optical scanner.
Environmental Survival negation for 72 hours.
When stationary, gain [Concealed] in any outdoor environment.
Hold Action for a free Ranged attack against the first enemy that moves into line of sight.
Move through occupied zones without triggering Engagement reactions, once per scene.

name: Smuggler's Trade
tagline: It's not contraband if no one finds it.
description: Concealment tools, encrypted comms, and a working knowledge of who to bribe and where to land without filing a flight plan.

benefits.description: A concealed carry system integrated into clothing. Evades casual searches.
Scrambled communications. Investigations must succeed at Difficulty 2 to intercept.
Once per session, retroactively declare that a piece of contraband was stored off-ship or with a contact.
Concealed carry system, evades casual searches.
Scrambled communications.
Generate convincing false manifests, IDs, and permits. Wits check to detect at Difficulty 3.
Once per session, retroactively declare contraband stored off-ship or with a contact.
In any port, gain access to Restricted-availability gear without requiring special contacts.

name: Slicer's Deck
tagline: Every lock is a polite suggestion.
description: Custom intrusion hardware, bypass tools, and the kind of software that makes Imperial network admins wake up screaming.

benefits.description: Portable intrusion hardware. Required for electronic bypass and network access attempts.
Electronic locks and basic security doors take a Maneuver (not an Action) to bypass.
Advanced intrusion suite. +1 Effect Tier on all Tech slicing rolls.
Single-use hardware override. Automatically succeed a basic security bypass without a roll. Consumable.
Electronic locks take a Maneuver to bypass.
After slicing a system, you may choose to leave no trace. The breach is undetectable until the next scheduled security sweep.

name: Enforcer's Rig
tagline: Persuasion is just violence with better PR.
description: Heavy armour, impact weapons, and the practiced intimidation of someone who has made people very sorry very quickly.

benefits.description: Reinforced clothing. Reduces incoming damage by 1.
Melee weapon. Damage: Physique+1. Upgrades unarmed strikes.
When you succeed on an Intimidate roll, the target immediately loses their next Minor Action.
Ballistic-resistant vest. Reduces incoming damage by 2.
Melee weapon. Damage: Physique+1.
Restraints. Restrain a Prone or Grappled target with a Brawl Maneuver.
Succeed on Intimidate: target loses their next Minor Action.
Once per encounter, after landing a Brawl hit, the target is immediately [Prone] regardless of Effect Tier.

name: Pilot's Loadout
tagline: Atmosphere is optional. Style is mandatory.
description: Flight gear, nav tools, and a relationship with your ship that borders on inappropriate but keeps everyone alive.

benefits.description: Integrated targeting display. +1 Effect Tier on Piloting rolls when in the cockpit.
Pre-loaded jump coordinates for three established routes.
Once per scene, push a Piloting roll to the Superior Effect Tier at no cost.
Integrated targeting display. +1 Effect Tier on Piloting rolls in cockpit.
Pre-loaded coordinates for six established routes, including two restricted corridors.
Once per session, negate one Critical Hit to your vehicle by burning the override. Consumable.
Once per scene, push a Piloting roll to Superior Effect at no cost.
You may perform full-speed turns and reverse-thrust maneuvers that would normally require a Difficulty check without rolling.

name: Scholar's Archive
tagline: Knowledge is power. Power has a price. You've read the receipt.
description: Research datapads, xenobiology references, and a catalogued mental library that comes in useful in the strangest situations.

benefits.description: Comprehensive reference archive. Negates penalties for unfamiliar topics on Investigation and Tactics rolls.
Once per scene, ask the GM one factual question about any organization, species, location, or piece of technology. The GM must answer honestly.
Comprehensive reference archive.
+1 Effect Tier on Medicine and Survival rolls involving non-Human biology.
Once per scene, ask the GM one factual question. The GM answers honestly.
After observing an enemy for one full round, identify one of their Weaknesses or Resistances without a roll.

name: Hunter's Package
tagline: They always run. They never get far.
description: Tracking hardware, restraints, and a methodical patience for turning a name on a contract into a body in a cargo hold.

benefits.description: A passive tracking device, embeds in a target's clothing or gear undetected on a successful Skulduggery roll.
Heavy restraints. Requires a Physique or Brawl roll at Difficulty 2 to escape.
At the start of a scene, designate one target as your Mark. Gain +1 Effect Tier on all rolls made to track, observe, or capture them.
Passive tracking device, embeds undetected on successful Skulduggery.
Heavy restraints.
Ranged Difficulty 1. On hit, target gains [Stunned] for 2 rounds. Consumable.
Designate one Mark per scene. +1 Effect Tier on rolls to track, observe, or capture them.
When your Mark attempts to flee or disengage, you may immediately take a free Move Maneuver toward them.

name: Force Adept's Focus
tagline: You feel something the others don't. Try not to call attention to it.
description: Meditation tools, handwritten instruction scraps from a dead master, and the discipline to avoid lighting up every Force-detector in the sector.

benefits.description: A personal focus object — crystal, pendant, or carved bone. Provides Advantage on Control rolls made during non-combat meditation.
You may always attempt Sense rolls to detect Force-sensitive individuals, active Force powers, or vergences — without requiring a Maneuver to focus.
Personal focus object. Advantage on Control rolls during meditation.
Fragmentary teachings. Once per session, reroll one failed Force discipline check.
Always attempt Sense rolls without a focusing Maneuver.
Your Force signature is muted. Passive detection attempts by Inquisitors or other Sensitives are made at Difficulty 3 instead of 1.
